ops.title: capt-hook guard pack: fail-open doctrine (2026-07-23)
ops.body:
Owner directive after the fourth over-aggression incident (`FISH_BIN=$(command -v fish); grep -aoE '…' "$FISH_BIN" | head` blocked despite the sink): "don't just bandaid — clean up this hook and pack a lot, simplify it, remove all the random edge case handling, it's ok to fail open."

**Doctrine (pack-wide, supersedes the fail-closed posture of rounds 1878eab / 903d2c0 / 5889bd8):**
1. A guard fires only on a positively identified offense; every ambiguity (unparseable flags, unstattable operands, variables, substitutions, env prefixes, untrusted cwd) falls through to ALLOW. Parsing gates only rewrites, never blocks.
2. Any downstream pipe → allow (the sink-quality walk — bounded head/tail/wc/sed/awk, tee defeats, null-data — deleted wholesale).
3. Policy steers stay, textual only: transcript operands → cc-transcript, hidden-segment operands → dep-reader; they fire even through pipes.
4. ccx-unresolvable infra fallbacks never block; feature-gap blocks (ccx can't express it) become allows.
5. approval_guards.py is exempt — its fail-closed shape is security posture (ambiguity → Ask dialog), not friction.

**Accepted residuals (signed off explicitly via AskUserQuestion):** piped floods (`grep -rz x . | head`, `| sort`); `$VAR` operands hiding transcript/.venv paths from the textual steers; big-file greps with no size cap; stderr spam from out-of-repo tree traversal (`grep -r x / | head`); flag-decorated `git diff`/`git show` variants running raw (only canonical bare forms rewrite).

An adversarial review documented these escapes before the owner accepted them — do not re-add lanes to close them without a fresh owner decision. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/bash-fish-bin-command-v-fish-logical-crown.md.
